summary: Multimedia content app 'Dard-e-munasif' undergoing complete rebuild from scratch due to persistent 'failed to load content' errors across Poetry, Dua, and Songs sections. Backend Motoko stable variables and query functions (getAllPoetry, getAllDua, getAllSongs) will be rewritten with minimal, clean implementation. All existing features will be preserved: four-category navigation, admin panel (PIN: 09186114), full-screen modals, hybrid music player, Web Audio API filters, hamburger menu with History/Downloads/Volume Manager, IndexedDB storage, and external links.
architectural_notes: Content categorization system: Dua, Poetry, Songs, Mix
Audio streaming without download capability for Songs
File upload system for images and audio files
Auto-generation of like counts (1,000-2,000 range) for social proof
Backend data persistence for all content types
Backend upload functions had redundant admin authorization check separate from PIN auth - fix: remove backend admin check and rely on PIN-protected frontend guard
IndexedDB used for History (metadata) and Downloads (audio Blobs) for offline access
Web Audio API pipeline for audio filters: Standard (raw), Clean Sound (High-Pass + 3kHz gain), Ultra Clean Sound (Band-Pass + Compressor)
Full app rebuild initiated — backend Motoko rewritten from scratch with minimal stable variables and simple query functions (no complex types, no auth checks on queries)
known_issues: Poetry section fails to load with 'failed to load poetry try again later' error - backend query issue
Admin panel song upload opens voice recorder instead of device file manager — input needs accept='audio/*' and capture attribute removed
Admin panel delete function not working — post remains visible after delete click; missing state refresh and confirm dialog
Backend delete functions (deletePoetry, deleteDua, deleteSong) returning 'Only admins can delete' error — authorization check must be removed
Audio plays silently on Android — likely OS-level permission or audio focus not requested; app does not route to Media/Music stream
App stuck on loading screen — taking a long time to open; likely backend canister initialization delay or route guard backend checks (getUserByDeviceId/getMaintenanceMode) hanging on slow network
Admin panel shows 'Only admin can set maintenance mode' error when toggling maintenance mode — backend setMaintenanceMode enforcing unauthorized identity check that must be removed
Admin panel Users & Controls tab shows 'Failed to load users, make sure you are admin' — backend getAllUsers enforcing unauthorized identity check that must be removed
Admin panel block/unblock user shows 'Only admin can block users' error — backend blockUser/unblockUser enforcing unauthorized identity check that must be removed

existing_features.title: Create main navigation with four categories: Dua (spiritual prayers), Poetry (poetic text/images), Songs (relaxing music/sounds), and Mix (combined feed showing trending content from all three categories). Homepage must display Mix section by default.
Implement Poetry posts with text/image content display, Download button, Like button, and auto-generated random like counts between 1,000-2,000 for social proof. Display category tag on each post.
Implement Dua posts with text/audio content display, Download button, Like button, and auto-generated random like counts between 1,000-2,000 for social proof. Display category tag on each post.
Implement Song posts with Play/Pause audio player controls only. Completely disable and hide download option for all song/audio posts. Display category tag on each post.
Create a secured /admin route hidden from normal navigation. Implement PIN authentication that ONLY accepts the exact PIN '09186114'. Do not allow any access to admin panel without correct PIN entry.
Implement admin panel functionality to upload new Poetry content with text/image, title, and content fields. Assign Poetry category automatically.
Implement admin panel functionality to upload new Dua content with text/audio, title, and content fields. Assign Dua category automatically.
Implement admin panel functionality to upload new Songs with audio files, title, and artist fields. Assign Songs category automatically.
Implement admin panel content management interface to view all posts and delete or manage existing content across all categories.

Design frontend with relaxing aesthetic using minimalist pastel colors or elegant dark mode option, modern calming typography, smooth animations and transitions, and responsive design for mobile and desktop.
Implement audio playback functionality for Dua and Song posts with proper streaming and play/pause controls.
Support display of Urdu and Hindi content in all text fields (titles, content, artist names) with proper Unicode rendering.

Investigate and fix the /admin route so it displays correctly. Verify that the route is properly registered in the TanStack Router configuration in App.tsx and that the AdminPage component is being rendered without errors.
Ensure the PINAuthGuard component in AdminPage.tsx is functioning correctly and properly validates the PIN '09186114' before showing the admin panel content.
Verify that all admin panel components (ContentManagement, PoetryUploadForm, DuaUploadForm, SongUploadForm) are properly imported and rendered without breaking the page.
Check for any routing conflicts, missing imports, or JavaScript errors that might prevent the admin panel from rendering. Review browser console logs and network requests for any failures.
Ensure all admin panel components (PINAuthGuard, ContentManagement, PoetryUploadForm, DuaUploadForm, SongUploadForm) are correctly imported and render without breaking the page
Verify that the /admin route is properly registered in the TanStack Router configuration in App.tsx with correct path and component mappings to ensure AdminPage renders without errors
Ensure the PINAuthGuard component in AdminPage.tsx correctly validates the hardcoded PIN '09186114' and properly manages authentication state in sessionStorage before rendering admin panel content

Verify that all admin panel components (ContentManagement, PoetryUploadForm, DuaUploadForm, SongUploadForm) are correctly imported in AdminPage.tsx with proper import paths and render without breaking the page

Investigate and resolve any routing conflicts, missing imports, or JavaScript runtime errors that prevent the admin panel from rendering by checking browser console logs, network requests, and component lifecycle errors
Ensure the admin panel page at /admin is accessible by direct URL navigation and displays the full admin interface including PIN authentication, content upload forms, and content management sections

Update the frontend upload mutation hooks (useCreatePoetry, useCreateDua, useCreateSong in useQueries.ts) and their corresponding form components (PoetryUploadForm, DuaUploadForm, SongUploadForm) to ensure they do not pass any admin token or special authorization header that could be mismatched or missing, relying solely on the anonymous actor call after PIN authentication passes.

Update the app name from 'Spiritual Hub' to 'Dard-e-munasif' everywhere it appears in the frontend — including the navbar/header title text, browser tab title (index.html <title>), and any other visible branding locations.

Replace the current logo/icon in the navbar, header, and favicon with the uploaded circular black & white logo image (dard-e-munasif-logo.dim_200x200.png) saved to frontend/public/assets/generated/. Display it as a circular image next to the app name in the header and set it as the browser favicon.

Fix the useGetAllPoetry React Query hook in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts to correctly call the backend actor's poetry fetch method, handle errors gracefully, and ensure the query key, actor call, and data transformation are all correct so the Poetry page renders content instead of the error state.

Verify and fix the PoetryPage component (frontend/src/pages/PoetryPage.tsx) to ensure the error boundary and loading skeleton states work correctly, and that the error alert is not triggered due to incorrect data shape mapping (e.g., mismatched field names between backend response and frontend type expectations).

When a user clicks on any post card in the Poetry or Dua category pages (or in the Mix feed), open a full-screen overlay modal displaying the full post content (title, image/audio, content text). Place a prominent ❌ close button at the top-right corner. Clicking ❌ dismisses the modal and returns the user to the feed without any page navigation.

Implement a hybrid Music Player for the Songs category. In the feed (SongsPage and MixPage), keep the existing small inline Play/Pause button on each song card for quick preview. When a user clicks anywhere on the song card except the inline Play/Pause button, open a full-screen 'Now Playing' UI featuring: album art placeholder, song title and artist name, a progress seeker bar (scrubable), Play/Pause button, elapsed/total time display, and a 'Now Playing' aesthetic. The full-screen player must be dismissible with a ❌ or down-arrow button.

Implement Web Audio API-based audio processing filters integrated into the Volume Manager. Three modes must produce real audio effects: (1) Standard — no filter, raw audio passthrough; (2) Clean Sound — apply a BiquadFilterNode High-Pass filter (cutoff ~200Hz) plus a BiquadFilterNode Peaking EQ at 3kHz with a positive gain boost (~4dB) to enhance voice/lyric clarity; (3) Ultra Clean Sound — apply a BiquadFilterNode Band-Pass filter plus a DynamicsCompressorNode to normalize volume and produce crisp, compressed output. The selected filter must apply to all audio playback across Song and Dua audio players.

Add a persistent Hamburger Menu (3-line icon) in the upper-right corner of the app header/navbar that is visible on all pages. The menu must contain three sections: (1) History — displays recently opened posts and songs (title, category, timestamp), stored as metadata in IndexedDB; (2) Downloads — lists audio files the user has downloaded, stored as Blobs in IndexedDB, with Play and Delete options for offline playback; (3) Volume Manager — contains a master volume slider (0–100%) and three mutually-exclusive filter toggle buttons: Standard, Clean Sound, Ultra Clean Sound. The hamburger menu must persist its open/close state appropriately across page navigation without full re-mount.

Implement IndexedDB storage utilities for History and Downloads. For History: on every post/song open event (modal open or full-screen player open), save metadata {id, title, category, timestamp} to an IndexedDB object store named 'history'. Display the 50 most recent entries in the History section. For Downloads: when a user downloads audio from a Dua post, save the audio Blob plus metadata {id, title, category} to an IndexedDB object store named 'downloads'. Allow offline playback of downloaded audio directly from IndexedDB Blob URLs. Provide a delete function to remove individual downloaded items.

Add a 'Be a Writer' button and a 'Contact' button in the hamburger menu (or as accessible footer/menu items visible on all pages). 'Be a Writer' must open the URL https://wa.me/919541525891?text=Join%20%E2%80%A2%7C-%C2%B0-%7C%E2%80%A2Program.Writter in a new tab. 'Contact' must open mailto:dariqlas744@gmail.com in the default mail client (or compose link). Both buttons must be clearly labeled.

Fix the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs React Query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts. Ensure each hook calls the correct backend actor method name exactly as defined in the Motoko actor, uses a stable query key, and correctly transforms the backend response (mapping Motoko record field names to frontend TypeScript type field names). Add graceful error handling so a failed fetch shows a user-friendly error state rather than crashing the UI.

Verify and fix the PoetryPage, DuaPage, and SongsPage components to ensure their loading skeleton states, error states, and empty states are triggered correctly based on the actual data shapes returned by the fixed hooks. Remove any code that incorrectly maps field names or makes incorrect type assumptions that could cause a valid response to be treated as an error.

Fix the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs React Query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts. Ensure each hook calls the exact backend actor method name as defined in the Motoko actor, uses a stable query key, and correctly transforms the backend Candid response by mapping Motoko record field names to the matching TypeScript type field names. Add try/catch error handling so a failed fetch returns a user-friendly error state instead of crashing the UI.

Verify and fix PoetryPage, DuaPage, and SongsPage components so their loading, error, and empty states are triggered correctly based on the actual data shapes returned by the fixed hooks. Remove any field-name mismatches or incorrect type assumptions that cause a valid response to be rendered as an error state. Ensure content renders when the backend returns valid data.

Fix the MixPage component to correctly aggregate data from the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs hooks after the hook fixes in REQ-42. Ensure the combined trending feed renders content from all three categories when any category has available posts, and shows appropriate loading/error states.

Fix the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs React Query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts to call the exact actor method names as defined in the corrected Motoko actor, correctly transform the Candid response by mapping all Motoko record field names (including any BigInt id/likeCount conversions) to their matching TypeScript type field names, and add try/catch error handling so network or Candid decode failures produce a user-friendly error state rather than crashing the UI.

Fix the PoetryPage, DuaPage, SongsPage, and MixPage components so that: (1) the 'No contents available' empty state is only shown when the returned data array is confirmed empty (length === 0) after a successful fetch, (2) the error state is only shown when the hook reports isError=true, (3) the loading skeleton is shown while isLoading=true, and (4) content cards render correctly when the data array is non-empty. Remove any field-name mismatches or type coercions that cause valid data to be treated as empty or erroneous.

Rewrite frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts from scratch. Define useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs React Query hooks that each call the exact matching backend actor method (getAllPoetry, getAllDua, getAllSongs respectively). Each hook must wrap the actor call in a try/catch, use a stable string query key (e.g. ['poetry'], ['dua'], ['songs']), and transform the Candid response by converting all BigInt fields (id, likeCount) to Number before returning. On error, the hook must set isError=true with a user-readable message rather than throwing. Define useCreatePoetry, useCreateDua, useCreateSong mutation hooks that pass no admin token or authorization header — they rely solely on the anonymous actor call. Define useDeletePoetry, useDeleteDua, useDeleteSong mutation hooks. All hooks must use the actor from the useActor hook.

Rewrite PoetryPage, DuaPage, SongsPage, and MixPage so their loading, error, and empty states are driven exclusively by the hook flags: show loading skeleton when isLoading=true, show error UI only when isError=true, show empty state only when data is a confirmed empty array (length === 0) after a successful fetch, and render content cards when data is a non-empty array. Remove all field-name mismatches, incorrect type coercions, and any logic that could treat valid data as an error or empty state. MixPage must aggregate the arrays from all three hooks, sort combined items by likeCount descending, and render the unified trending feed.

Ensure all existing app features are preserved in the rebuild: (1) Four-category navigation (Mix as default homepage, Poetry, Dua, Songs) in Layout/Navbar. (2) Poetry posts with text/image display, Download button, Like button, random like counts 1000–2000 seeded on first load if likeCount is 0. (3) Dua posts with text/audio display, Download button (saves to IndexedDB and browser), Like button. (4) Song posts with inline Play/Pause only — download option completely hidden. (5) Full-screen overlay modal for Poetry and Dua post cards with ❌ close button. (6) Hybrid Music Player for Songs: inline Play/Pause on card, full-screen Now Playing UI (album art placeholder, seeker bar, elapsed/total time, Play/Pause) when card body is clicked. (7) Web Audio API filters in Volume Manager: Standard (no filter), Clean Sound (High-Pass ~200Hz + Peaking EQ 3kHz +4dB), Ultra Clean Sound (Band-Pass + DynamicsCompressor). (8) Hamburger menu (top-right, persistent) with History (50 most recent entries from IndexedDB), Downloads (audio Blobs from IndexedDB with Play/Delete), and Volume Manager (master volume slider + filter toggles). (9) 'Be a Writer' button opening https://wa.me/919541525891?text=Join%20%E2%80%A2%7C-%C2%B0-%7C%E2%80%A2Program.Writter in new tab. (10) 'Contact' button opening mailto:dariqlas744@gmail.com. (11) PIN-protected /admin route accepting only PIN '09186114', with upload forms for Poetry, Dua, Songs and full content management (view/delete). (12) App name 'Dard-e-munasif' in header, navbar, and browser tab title. (13) Circular logo image (dard-e-munasif-logo.dim_200x200.png) displayed in header and set as favicon. (14) Urdu/Hindi Unicode rendering in all text fields. (15) Relaxing aesthetic with dark mode, pastel/calm color palette, smooth animations, and responsive mobile/desktop layout.

In the SongUploadForm component (frontend/src/components/admin/SongUploadForm.tsx), update the audio file input element so it opens the device's local file manager/music library instead of the voice recorder. Set the input's `accept` attribute to `audio/*` (or explicitly `audio/mpeg,audio/mp3,audio/wav,audio/ogg,audio/aac,audio/*`) and remove any `capture` attribute that may be causing the voice recorder to open. The input must allow the user to browse and select MP3 and other audio files directly from their device storage.

Fix the delete functionality in the ContentManagement component (frontend/src/components/admin/ContentManagement.tsx) so that: (1) A 'Confirm Deletion' dialog/popup appears when the user clicks any Delete button, asking the user to confirm before proceeding. (2) Upon confirmation, the delete mutation (useDeletePoetry, useDeleteDua, or useDeleteSong) is called with the correct post ID. (3) After a successful delete response from the backend, the React Query cache for the relevant category is immediately invalidated so the UI automatically re-fetches and removes the deleted post from the displayed list without requiring a manual page refresh. (4) If the delete call fails, show a user-friendly error toast notification.

Fix the audio player in frontend/src/hooks/useAudioPlayback.ts and frontend/src/components/AudioPlayer.tsx so that audio actually produces sound during playback. Specifically: (1) Ensure the HTMLAudioElement is never muted by default — set audioElement.muted = false explicitly on initialization. (2) Ensure the audio element's volume is initialized to the current master volume value (defaulting to 1.0 / 100% if no stored value exists) rather than 0. (3) Ensure the Web Audio API filter chain in useWebAudioFilters.ts correctly connects the audio source node through the filter graph to the AudioContext destination — a broken or disconnected filter graph silently drops audio output; fall back to direct output if Web Audio API is unavailable. (4) Ensure the audio element's src is set correctly from the Blob URL or data URL before play() is called, and that the format (audio/mpeg, audio/mp3, audio/aac, audio/wav, audio/ogg) is supported by setting the correct MIME type. (5) On play(), call audioContext.resume() first if the AudioContext state is 'suspended' (required by browser autoplay policies) before calling audioElement.play().

Rewrite useAudioPlayback.ts to eliminate all silent-audio causes: (1) Set HTMLAudioElement.muted = false and HTMLAudioElement.volume = 1.0 unconditionally on every new audio element creation — never read from any stored volume preference or volume state variable. (2) Remove all references to any volume state, volumeLevel, masterVolume, or similar variables that could set volume to a value other than 1.0. The audio element volume must always be hard-coded to 1.0. (3) Remove all Web Audio API filter chain connections (useWebAudioFilters integration) from the playback path — bypass all filter nodes and connect the audio source directly to the AudioContext destination, or skip the AudioContext entirely and play via the raw HTMLAudioElement to eliminate any broken filter graph that silently drops audio output. (4) Before calling audioElement.play(), always call audioContext.resume() if the AudioContext exists and its state is 'suspended' to satisfy browser autoplay policies.

Rewrite NowPlayingModal.tsx and SongPost.tsx to also enforce volume = 1.0 and muted = false on any audio element they directly create or reference. Remove any code in these components that reads a volume value from context, props, or localStorage and applies it to the audio element. Ensure the audio src is set from the correct Blob URL before play() is called, and that the MIME type used when constructing the Blob URL matches the stored audioMimeType field from the backend (e.g. audio/mpeg for MP3, audio/aac for AAC).

Add a buffering health check to the audio playback flow in useAudioPlayback.ts, NowPlayingModal.tsx, and AudioPlayer.tsx: attach a listener to the HTMLAudioElement 'waiting' and 'stalled' events. If the audio element fires 'stalled' or if after 5 seconds of a play() call the buffered TimeRanges are empty (audio.buffered.length === 0), display a visible notification/toast message to the user: 'Audio data not found.' This check must not interrupt or pause otherwise-working playback.

Integrate the Screen Wake Lock API into the audio playback lifecycle in useAudioPlayback.ts: when audio playback starts (play() is called successfully), request a wake lock using navigator.wakeLock.request('screen') to prevent the device from sleeping and silencing audio. When audio playback is paused, stopped, or the component unmounts, release the wake lock. Wrap the entire Wake Lock integration in a try/catch — if the browser does not support the Wake Lock API, silently skip it without throwing an error or showing any warning to the user.

Remove the entire HamburgerMenu Volume Manager section (master volume slider and filter toggle buttons) from HamburgerMenu.tsx, and remove all references to volume state, volume context, masterVolume, volumeLevel, and filter mode selection (Standard / Clean Sound / Ultra Clean Sound) from HamburgerMenu.tsx and useWebAudioFilters.ts. Do not remove the History and Downloads sections of the hamburger menu. The audio engine must play all audio at a fixed volume of 1.0 with no user-controllable volume setting in the UI.

In useAudioPlayback.ts, before initiating any audio playback, use the Web Permissions API (navigator.permissions.query / navigator.mediaDevices or equivalent browser API) to check and request permission for audio/media playback. If the browser supports the 'microphone' or 'autoplay' permission query, check its state. On browsers/environments where the Permissions API reports 'denied', set a React state flag (e.g. permissionDenied=true). Wrap the entire permission request in a try/catch so it silently skips on unsupported browsers. This simulates the Android 'Allow Music and Audio' (API 33+) / 'READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE' permission request flow in the web context.

In useAudioPlayback.ts, simulate an OS Audio Focus request by calling AudioContext.resume() immediately when the user initiates playback, and additionally dispatch a user-activation gesture guard so the AudioContext transitions from 'suspended' to 'running' before audioElement.play() is called. Log a console message 'Audio focus requested' when AudioContext.resume() is called. This ensures the browser/OS audio subsystem treats the app as an active media session rather than silencing it.

In useAudioPlayback.ts, SongPost.tsx, NowPlayingModal.tsx, and AudioPlayer.tsx: if the permissionDenied flag from REQ-64 is true (or if the AudioContext cannot be created or resumed), do not attempt playback. Instead, expose a boolean isPeermissionDenied (or similar) from the hook and display a visible inline message to the user: 'Please allow audio permissions in settings to hear the music.' This message must appear wherever the play/pause control is rendered — in the song card (SongPost.tsx), the Now Playing modal (NowPlayingModal.tsx), and the Dua audio player (AudioPlayer.tsx).

In useAudioPlayback.ts and AudioPlayer.tsx, set the HTMLAudioElement to target the media/music audio output channel by applying the following: (1) Set audio.setAttribute('x-webkit-airplay', 'allow') for Safari/iOS compatibility. (2) Ensure no Web Audio API nodes route output to a gain node with value 0 or to a disconnected destination — the audio graph must terminate at AudioContext.destination. (3) Do not set any stream type that maps to a system UI or ringer channel. (4) If the MediaSession API is available (navigator.mediaSession), set navigator.mediaSession.metadata with the song title and artist so the OS registers the app as a media player on the lock screen/notification shade, which routes audio through the media stream rather than the ringer stream.

In useAudioPlayback.ts, NowPlayingModal.tsx, SongPost.tsx, and AudioPlayer.tsx: immediately after a successful AudioContext.resume() and before audioElement.play() is called, unconditionally set audioElement.volume = 1.0 and audioElement.muted = false. Additionally, if the MediaSession API is available, set navigator.mediaSession.playbackState = 'playing' after play() resolves. These assignments must happen on every play() invocation, not just on first initialization, to prevent any OS-level or software volume reset from silencing audio.

Create a LoginPage component (frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx) with a centered layout matching the app's existing dark/pastel aesthetic. It must contain: (1) A text input field labeled 'Enter Your Name' that accepts the user's display name. (2) A 'Connect with Server' button that, when clicked with a non-empty name, reveals two server option buttons: 'V.99.kashmir.2HZ.World' and 'Developer.99Hz/ikhlas.Java'. (3) When the user clicks either server option, store the entered name and selected server in localStorage (keys: 'dmUser_name' and 'dmUser_server') and navigate to the home page (/). (4) The name input must be validated — the 'Connect with Server' button must be disabled or show an inline error if the name field is empty. (5) The server selection step must only appear after the name is submitted via the button click.

Register a /login route in App.tsx pointing to the new LoginPage component. Implement a route guard: on every route render in App.tsx (or in the Layout component), check if localStorage keys 'dmUser_name' and 'dmUser_server' are both present and non-empty. If either is missing, redirect the user to /login immediately. The /login route itself must be accessible without the guard applying to it. All other existing routes (/, /poetry, /dua, /songs, /admin) must be protected by this guard — users cannot reach them without completing the login flow.

On the LoginPage (frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx), after the user enters their name and selects a server, before navigating to the home page: (1) Generate or retrieve a Device ID (HWID) for the browser using a fingerprint composed of navigator.userAgent + screen.width + screen.height + navigator.language + navigator.hardwareConcurrency, hashed or concatenated into a stable string, and stored in localStorage under key 'dmUser_deviceId' so it persists across sessions. (2) Call the backend registerUser(name, server, deviceId) actor method. (3) Store the returned uniqueCode in localStorage under key 'dmUser_uniqueCode'. (4) Also store the deviceId in localStorage under key 'dmUser_deviceId' if not already stored. (5) After successful registration, navigate to the home page as before. Wrap the actor call in a try/catch — if the call fails, show an inline error message and do not navigate.

On the Home Page (MixPage or the root '/' route component rendered by Layout), add a display section at the very top center of the page content area (above the feed grid): (1) Show the user's Unique Code from localStorage ('dmUser_uniqueCode') in a styled read-only text box or chip — it must be selectable and copyable (e.g. via a copy-to-clipboard button or selectable text) but must not be editable (use readOnly input or non-input display with user-select: all). (2) Directly below the Unique Code, display the user's Name from localStorage ('dmUser_name') in a slightly smaller, calming typography style matching the app's dark/pastel aesthetic. Both elements must be visible on all screen sizes.

In the app's route guard logic (Layout.tsx or App.tsx), add a maintenance mode and ban check that runs on every route render for all protected routes: (1) On app load (and before rendering any page content), call the backend query getUserByDeviceId(deviceId) using the deviceId from localStorage ('dmUser_deviceId'). If the returned user record has isBlocked=true, immediately redirect to a full-screen Ban Screen component (do not render any app content). (2) Also call the backend query getMaintenanceMode(). If it returns true, immediately redirect to a full-screen Maintenance Screen component (do not render any app content). (3) These checks must happen before the main content renders — show a brief loading indicator while the checks are in progress. (4) Both checks must re-run on every page navigation so a blocked or maintenance-mode user cannot bypass the screen by navigating to another route. (5) If the backend call fails (e.g. network error), allow the user through rather than blocking them, to avoid false positives.

Create a BanScreen component (frontend/src/components/BanScreen.tsx) that renders a full-screen centered layout matching the app's dark aesthetic. It must display: a prominent warning icon or visual element, the message 'Your account has been suspended. Please contact support.', and no navigation links, back buttons, or any other app content. The component must fill the entire viewport and prevent any interaction with the rest of the app.

Create a MaintenanceScreen component (frontend/src/components/MaintenanceScreen.tsx) that renders a full-screen centered layout matching the app's dark aesthetic. It must display exactly the following message: 'App is currently under maintenance for a better experience. We will be back online shortly – Developer Ikhlas.' No navigation links, back buttons, or any other app content should be present. The component must fill the entire viewport.

In the Admin Panel (frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx), add a new 'Users & Controls' tab alongside the existing content tabs. This tab must contain: (1) A 'Maintenance Mode' toggle switch labeled clearly as 'Maintenance Mode'. When toggled ON, it calls the backend setMaintenanceMode(true); when toggled OFF, it calls setMaintenanceMode(false). The toggle must reflect the current state by calling getMaintenanceMode() on tab load. Show a success toast when the state is changed. (2) A user list table below the toggle that calls getAllUsers() to display all registered users. Each row must show: Name, Server, Unique Code, Device ID (truncated), and a 'Block' button. Clicking 'Block' calls blockUser(uniqueCode) and refreshes the list. If a user is already blocked, show an 'Unblock' button instead that calls unblockUser(uniqueCode). Show a confirmation dialog before blocking or unblocking.

Add React Query hooks to frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts for the new user management backend functions: (1) useRegisterUser — a mutation hook that calls the backend registerUser(name, server, deviceId) method and returns the uniqueCode. (2) useGetUserByDeviceId — a query hook that calls getUserByDeviceId(deviceId) and returns the optional user record. (3) useGetMaintenanceMode — a query hook that calls getMaintenanceMode() and returns a boolean. (4) useSetMaintenanceMode — a mutation hook that calls setMaintenanceMode(enabled). (5) useGetAllUsers — a query hook that calls getAllUsers() and returns the array of user records. (6) useBlockUser — a mutation hook that calls blockUser(uniqueCode). (7) useUnblockUser — a mutation hook that calls unblockUser(uniqueCode). All hooks must use the actor from the useActor hook and follow the same error-handling and query-key patterns as the existing hooks.

Fix the UsersControlsPanel component (frontend/src/components/admin/UsersControlsPanel.tsx) so that the user list table correctly fetches and renders all registered users by properly calling the useGetAllUsers hook. Ensure the table displays each user's Name, Server, Unique Code, Device ID (truncated), isBlocked status, and Block/Unblock buttons. The table must show data as soon as the 'Users & Controls' tab is active, with a loading skeleton while fetching and an empty-state message if no users are registered yet.

Add a search/filter input bar above the users table in the UsersControlsPanel component (frontend/src/components/admin/UsersControlsPanel.tsx) labeled 'Search by Unique ID'. As the admin types into this field, the displayed user rows must be filtered client-side to only show entries whose Unique Code contains the entered string (case-insensitive). Clearing the input restores the full unfiltered list.

In Layout.tsx, add a hard timeout to the route guard's backend checks (getUserByDeviceId and getMaintenanceMode). If either backend call does not resolve within 4 seconds, treat the result as non-blocking and allow the user through to the requested route rather than keeping them on an infinite loading screen. The timeout must be implemented per-call using Promise.race() with a timeout promise. On timeout, log a warning to the console but do not show any error to the user — simply render the app content as normal.

In Layout.tsx, change the route guard so both getUserByDeviceId and getMaintenanceMode backend calls run in parallel using Promise.allSettled() instead of sequentially. This reduces total blocking time from the sum of both call durations to the maximum of the two. If either call rejects or is rejected by allSettled, treat it as non-blocking (allow user through) rather than blocking or erroring.

In Layout.tsx, if localStorage keys 'dmUser_deviceId', 'dmUser_name', or 'dmUser_server' are missing or empty when the route guard runs, skip the getUserByDeviceId and getMaintenanceMode backend calls entirely and redirect immediately to /login without showing any loading indicator. Do not attempt backend calls when the user is not yet registered, as these calls will always fail or hang and are the primary cause of the infinite loading screen for first-time users.

Replace the full-page white 'Loading...' screen shown during route guard checks in Layout.tsx with a styled loading indicator matching the app's dark aesthetic. Display the app logo (dard-e-munasif-logo.dim_200x200.png) centered on screen with a subtle pulsing animation and the text 'Loading...' below it, on a dark background consistent with the app's color palette. This ensures the loading state feels intentional and branded rather than showing a blank white screen.

In frontend/src/components/admin/UsersControlsPanel.tsx, remove the error message text 'Failed to load users. Make sure you are logged in as admin.' and replace it with a generic error message such as 'Failed to load users. Please try again.' so it no longer misleads the admin after the backend authorization check is removed.

In frontend/src/components/admin/UsersControlsPanel.tsx, replace the error message text 'Failed to load users. Make sure you are logged in as admin.' with 'Failed to load users. Please try again.' so it no longer displays the misleading admin login requirement message.

new_feature_requests.summary: Main navigation with four categories: Dua, Poetry, Songs, and Mix (default homepage showing trending content from all categories)
Poetry & Dua posts with Download button, Like button, and auto-generated like counts (1,000-2,000)
Song posts with Play/Pause audio player only (download completely disabled and hidden)
Secured /admin route with PIN authentication (PIN: 09186114) - no access without correct PIN
Admin panel features: upload Poetry (text/image), Dua (text/audio), Songs (audio), assign categories, delete/manage content, view all posts
Relaxing aesthetic UI with minimalist pastel colors or elegant dark mode, modern typography, smooth animations, responsive design
Backend with data persistence, file uploads (images/audio), audio playback, secure PIN validation, Urdu/Hindi content support

Implement backend data models and persistence for storing Poetry (text, image, title, content, category, like count), Dua (text, audio, title, content, category, like count), and Songs (audio, title, artist, category) with proper file upload handling for images and audio files.

Remove or bypass the backend admin authorization check in the Motoko main actor for the createPoetry, createDua, and createSong functions so that any caller can successfully upload content. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the only authentication gate required — the backend should not reject upload calls with 'Unauthorized: Only admins can create poetry' (or equivalent messages for Dua/Song). All three upload functions must accept requests without requiring a separate admin identity or token.
Rename app from 'Spiritual Hub' to 'Dard-e-munasif' and replace branding with the uploaded black & white circular logo (blindfolded figure) across navbar, header, and favicon.

Investigate and fix the backend Motoko actor's getAllPoetry (or equivalent) query function to ensure it returns poetry entries correctly without errors or traps. Verify the function signature, return type, and that stored data is accessible without causing a rejection.
Full-screen modal for Poetry & Dua posts with ❌ close button to return to feed
Hybrid Music Player: inline Play/Pause on song cards in feed, full-screen Now Playing UI (album art, seeker bar, Play/Pause) when card is tapped
Web Audio API filters in Volume Manager: Standard (no filter), Clean Sound (High-Pass + 3kHz boost), Ultra Clean Sound (Band-Pass + Dynamics Compressor)
Hamburger menu (top right, persists across pages) with History (IndexedDB metadata), Downloads (IndexedDB audio Blobs for offline), and Volume Manager (master slider + filter toggles)
External redirects: 'Be a Writer' button → WhatsApp (https://wa.me/919541525891?text=Join%20•|-°-|•Program.Writter), 'Contact' → Gmail compose to dariqlas744@gmail.com

Audit the backend Motoko actor in backend/main.mo and verify that getAllPoetry, getAllDua, and getAllSongs (or their equivalent query functions) have correct function signatures, return types, and can be called without trapping or rejecting. Ensure each function returns an array of the correct record type with all expected fields (id, title, content, category, likeCount, and any media blob fields).

Audit the backend Motoko actor in backend/main.mo and verify that getAllPoetry, getAllDua, and getAllSongs query functions have correct function signatures, return types, and stable variable access. Ensure each function returns an Array of the correct record type with all expected fields (id, title, content, category, likeCount, and any media blob fields) without trapping or rejecting. Fix any issues found so all three query functions execute successfully.

Fix 'No contents available' issue: audit Motoko stable variables initialization and data persistence, verify getAllPoetry/getAllDua/getAllSongs return stored entries correctly, and fix frontend rendering logic distinguishing empty vs error states.

Audit the backend Motoko actor in backend/main.mo and verify that all stable variables (for poetry, dua, and songs) are correctly declared with the `stable` keyword, properly initialized, and that data written via createPoetry, createDua, and createSong is actually persisted to those stable stores. Fix any missing `stable` keyword, incorrect variable shadowing, or re-initialization logic that would cause stored entries to be lost or invisible to query functions.

Verify and fix the getAllPoetry, getAllDua, and getAllSongs query functions in backend/main.mo so each correctly iterates over the stable storage variable and returns all stored entries as an array. Ensure the return type annotation matches the actual record shape (id, title, content, category, likeCount, and media blob/reference fields) and that no trap or rejection occurs during iteration or field access.

Rewrite backend/main.mo from scratch with a minimal, clean Motoko actor. Declare three stable variables using TrieMap or a simple Array-backed approach: one each for poetry, dua, and songs. Each poetry entry must have fields: id (Nat), title (Text), content (Text), category (Text), likeCount (Nat), imageData (Blob), imageMimeType (Text). Each dua entry must have fields: id (Nat), title (Text), content (Text), category (Text), likeCount (Nat), audioData (Blob), audioMimeType (Text). Each song entry must have fields: id (Nat), title (Text), artist (Text), category (Text), likeCount (Nat), audioData (Blob), audioMimeType (Text). Expose public query functions getAllPoetry, getAllDua, getAllSongs that return arrays of the respective record types. Expose public update functions createPoetry, createDua, createSong with no authorization checks — any caller may invoke them. Expose deletePoetry, deleteDua, deleteSong by id. Expose incrementPoetryLike, incrementDuaLike, incrementSongLike by id. Use simple Nat counters for auto-incrementing IDs. Ensure all stable variables are declared with the `stable` keyword and are never re-initialized on upgrade.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the createPoetry, createDua, and createSong update functions. These functions must accept calls from any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning an 'Unauthorized' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on these three functions.
Fix admin panel song upload to open device file manager/local storage for MP3 selection instead of voice recorder. Input must accept audio files from device music library.
Fix admin panel delete function: ensure delete removes post from backend database, UI refreshes automatically after deletion, and add a 'Confirm Deletion' popup to prevent accidental deletes.

Audit the backend delete functions (deletePoetry, deleteDua, deleteSong) in backend/main.mo to ensure they correctly remove entries from their respective stable storage variables by ID and return a success/failure result. Verify that after a delete call the entry is no longer returned by getAllPoetry, getAllDua, or getAllSongs respectively.

Fix audio player: music shows as playing but no sound comes out. Ensure correct audio stream initialization, unmute by default, volume synced to 100%, MP3/AAC format support, and proper audio focus handling.

Fix 'Only admins can delete' error on delete: backend delete functions should not enforce admin/identity checks — any caller (including anonymous) must be able to delete content since the admin PIN gate is the sole auth.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the deletePoetry, deleteDua, and deleteSong functions. These functions must accept calls from any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning an 'Unauthorized: Only admins can delete' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on these three delete functions.

Deep fix audio engine: force HTMLAudioElement volume to 1.0, remove all leftover volume=0 variables, add buffering listener with 'Audio data not found' notification if buffer is empty, enable Wake Lock API to prevent processor sleep silencing audio, and ensure audio output targets media/music stream with no software mutes.

Fix silent audio via permissions and audio focus: request system-level audio/media permissions (Android 13+ 'Allow Music and Audio', older 'Read External Storage'), request OS Audio Focus, show permission guard message if denied, route audio to Media/Music stream (not System/Ringer), and hard-code initial playback volume to 1.0 immediately after permission granted.

Mandatory login page with 'Enter Your Name' input and 'Connect with Server' button. Shows two server options ('V.99.kashmir.2HZ.World' and 'Developer.99Hz/ikhlas.Java') after name entry. User must select a server to access the Home Page — no bypass allowed.

On login, generate a Unique Code for each user and capture their Device ID (HWID). Store both in the backend database linked to their name. On the Home Page, display the Unique Code at top center (copyable but not editable) and their Name directly below it.

Add a global 'Maintenance Mode' toggle in the Admin Panel. When ON, all users are redirected to a maintenance screen ('App is currently under maintenance for a better experience. We will be back online shortly – Developer Ikhlas.') and cannot access any app content until the admin turns it OFF.

In the Admin Panel, add a 'Block' button next to each user's Unique ID. Blocking a user must instantly log them out in real-time. Even after restart or reinstall, the app must recognize their Device ID (HWID) and keep them on a permanent Ban Screen.

In backend/main.mo, add the following new stable data structures and functions: (1) A stable variable to store user records with fields: uniqueCode (Text), deviceId (Text), name (Text), server (Text), isBlocked (Bool). (2) A stable variable for a single maintenanceMode flag (Bool), defaulting to false. (3) A public update function registerUser(name: Text, server: Text, deviceId: Text) that checks if a record with the given deviceId already exists — if yes, return the existing uniqueCode; if no, generate a new unique code (e.g. a short alphanumeric string derived from a Nat counter prefixed with 'DM-'), create a new user record with isBlocked=false, store it, and return the new uniqueCode. (4) A public query function getUserByDeviceId(deviceId: Text) returning an optional user record so the frontend can check block status and retrieve uniqueCode on app start. (5) A public query function getMaintenanceMode() returning Bool. (6) A public update function setMaintenanceMode(enabled: Bool) with no authorization check. (7) A public update function blockUser(uniqueCode: Text) that sets isBlocked=true for the matching user record. (8) A public update function unblockUser(uniqueCode: Text) that sets isBlocked=false. (9) A public query function getAllUsers() returning an array of all user records (uniqueCode, deviceId, name, server, isBlocked) for the Admin Panel user list.

Fix admin panel Users & Controls tab: user list table not showing registered/logged-in users, and missing search by Unique ID bar. Ensure getAllUsers() data renders in the table and add a search/filter input for Unique ID.

Verify the backend getAllUsers() query function in backend/main.mo correctly iterates over the stable users variable and returns all stored user records as an array. Ensure the return type matches the frontend's expected shape (uniqueCode: Text, deviceId: Text, name: Text, server: Text, isBlocked: Bool) and that no trap or rejection occurs. If any issue is found, fix it so the function reliably returns all registered users.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the setMaintenanceMode function. This function must accept calls from any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning an 'Unauthorized: Only admins can set maintenance mode' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on setMaintenanceMode.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the getAllUsers query function. This function must return all registered user records to any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning an 'Unauthorized' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on getAllUsers.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the getAllUsers query function. This function must return all registered user records to any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning an 'Unauthorized' or 'Make sure you are logged in as admin' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on getAllUsers.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the setMaintenanceMode function. This function must accept calls from any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning an 'Unauthorized: Only admins can set maintenance mode' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on setMaintenanceMode.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the blockUser and unblockUser functions. These functions must accept calls from any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning an 'Unauthorized: Only admins can block users' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the blockUser and unblockUser functions. These functions must accept calls from any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning an 'Unauthorized: Only admins can block users' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on these two functions.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the setMaintenanceMode function. This function must accept calls from any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning any 'Unauthorized: Only admins can set maintenance mode' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on setMaintenanceMode.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the getAllUsers query function. This function must return all registered user records to any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning any 'Unauthorized' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on getAllUsers.

In backend/main.mo, remove all authorization/identity checks from the blockUser and unblockUser functions. These functions must accept calls from any caller (including anonymous) without throwing or returning any 'Unauthorized: Only admins can block users' error. The PIN-protected frontend admin panel is the sole authentication gate — the backend must not enforce any caller identity restriction on these two functions.
